dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S4105 IS: Naturalization Accountability Act
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Cotton introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on the Judiciary

form.official-title: To include any felony conviction as a ground for revocation of naturalization, to strike the 5-year limitation on the revocation of naturalization for membership in certain totalitarian or treasonous organizations, and to eliminate the 10-year statute of limitation for certain criminal penalties that would disqualify a person from naturalization.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the Naturalization Accountability Act.
Section 340(c) of the Immigration and Nationality Act (8 U.S.C. 1451) is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking shall within five years next following such naturalization become and inserting becomes; and
by inserting or has been convicted at any time of any felony, after section 313,.

section.header: Elimination of 10-year statute of limitation for criminal penalties for procurement of citizenship or naturalization unlawfully
section.text: Section 3291 of title 18, United States Code, is amended—

paragraph.text: by striking No person and inserting the following:
by striking sections 1423 to 1428 and inserting sections 1423, 1424, and 1426 to 1428; and
by adding at the end the following:

subsection.text: Notwithstanding any other law, an indictment may be found or an information instituted at any time without limitation for any offense under section 1425.
